title: Gusto vs ADP
description: Detailed comparison of Gusto's modern all-in-one HR platform versus ADP's enterprise-grade payroll and workforce management solution.

features.name: Tax Filing & Compliance
features.tool1Detail: Auto filing for federal, state, and local
features.tool2Detail: Full tax compliance with W-2/1095-C support

features.name: Multi-State Payroll
features.tool1Detail: Up to 50 states with reciprocal agreements

features.name: Integrations
features.tool1Detail: Deep integration with QuickBooks, Xero, Slack

features.name: Customer Support Quality
features.tool2Detail: Varies by plan, enterprise gets priority

features.name: Pricing Transparency
features.tool2Detail: Custom quotes, often expensive for small biz

verdict: Gusto delivers a beautiful, intuitive all-in-one HR and payroll platform that small to mid-sized businesses can set up in minutes, with transparent pricing, automated tax filing, and integrated benefits that eliminate administrative headaches. ADP is the enterprise-grade juggernaut with global payroll coverage, advanced workforce analytics, and compliance support across 140+ countries that large organizations depend on. Companies under 500 employees will find Gusto's modern UX and exceptional support far more practical and cost-effective. Enterprises with complex multi-national operations need ADP's depth, but should prepare for a lengthy implementation and higher costs.

faqs.question: Which is better for a small business, Gusto or ADP?
faqs.answer: Gusto is strongly preferred for small businesses under 500 employees. It offers faster setup, simpler payroll processing, transparent pricing starting at $40/month plus $6/employee, and better customer support. ADP's small business solution (RUN) is functional but lacks the modern experience and ease of use that Gusto provides.

faqs.question: Can Gusto handle multi-state payroll?
faqs.answer: Yes, Gusto supports payroll in all 50 US states plus Washington DC and US territories. It handles reciprocal agreements between states and automatically applies the correct tax rules based on employee work location. However, ADP offers broader coverage for international employees across 140+ countries.

faqs.question: Is ADP worth the higher cost?
faqs.answer: ADP is worth the investment for organizations with 1,000+ employees, especially those operating internationally or requiring deep compliance support. For small to mid-sized businesses, Gusto provides 90% of the functionality at half the cost with significantly better ease of use.

faqs.question: Do both platforms integrate with QuickBooks?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Gusto and ADP integrate with QuickBooks. Gusto offers a native seamless sync that automatically pushes payroll journal entries to QuickBooks. ADP also connects via the QuickBooks App Store, though the setup is more technical and may require IT assistance to configure correctly.

faqs.question: Which has better benefits administration?
faqs.answer: Gusto offers a more streamlined benefits experience with integrated health insurance, 401(k), HSA/FSA, and workers' comp that employers can manage in one dashboard. ADP provides more customizable benefits administration including flexible spending accounts and COBRA management, but the setup and ongoing administration is more complex.
